# UC-22.57.7 · SG Cyber Act mandatory penetration test scheduling + remediation closure tracker

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Vulnerability &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Once a year, a licensed ethical hacker tries to break into our systems. We track every test and make sure any serious problems are fixed within 30 days.*

---

## Description

Tracks PT currency and remediation closure for critical findings.

## Value

Failure to PT or to close critical findings within 30 days is enforcement-actionable.

## Implementation

(1) Populate `csa_pt_lookup` after each PT. (2) Schedule UC-22.57.7 daily. (3) Build the `CSA PT Schedule + Remediation` dashboard.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.57.7` archived daily to `audit_evidence` with `csa_clause="CSA-CCoP-VM-PT"`.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Set `last_pt_date` to >400 days ago. UC-22.57.7 MUST flag.

### Negative scenario

PT within 365 days with critical findings closed within 30 days. UC-22.57.7 MUST NOT escalate.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

Same as UC-22.57.5.

## Step 1 — PT register

```ini
[csa_pt_lookup]
field.cii_id = string
field.last_pt_date = number
field.tester_id = string
field.tester_licence_id = string
field.report_doc_url = string
field.critical_findings_open = number
field.critical_findings_overdue = number
field.high_findings_open = number
```

## Step 2 — Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.57.7: CSA PT Schedule + Remediation]
cron_schedule = 11 13 * * *
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.57.7,reg=SG-Cyber-Act,clause=CSA-CCoP-VM-PT
alert.severity = 4
```

## Step 3 — Runbook

**`critical_findings_overdue > 0`**: close within 7 days; escalate to CC + CISO.

**SG-specific failure modes**:
- **Licensed tester required**: ensure tester is on the CSA licensee register.
- **Cross-CII scope reuse**: shared services may be tested once; ensure scope memo defines coverage.

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup csa_pt_lookup
| eval days_since_pt = round((now() - last_pt_date) / 86400, 0)
| eval pt_status = case(
    days_since_pt > 365, "overdue",
    days_since_pt > 335, "due_soon",
    critical_findings_overdue > 0, "critical_findings_overdue",
    1=1, "current")
| where pt_status IN ("overdue","due_soon","critical_findings_overdue")
| table cii_id last_pt_date days_since_pt tester_id tester_licence_id critical_findings_open critical_findings_overdue high_findings_open pt_status
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) PT currency by CII. (Panel 2) Open critical/high. (Panel 3) Tester licensing status. (Panel 4) 5-year PT history.

## Known False Positives

1. **Multi-CII shared tester**: a single tester may cover multiple CIIs; ensure per-CII tracking.
2. **Scope split**: PT may run in multiple phases; aggregate the latest finished phase.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `csa_22_57_7_exceptions`.

## References

- [CCoP 2.0 §VM-PT](https://www.csa.gov.sg/legislation/code-of-practice)
- [CSA Cybersecurity Service Provider licensing](https://www.csa.gov.sg/legislation/cybersecurity-service-provider-licensing)
